content.problemStatement:
On Republic Day, Chef wants to distribute ladoos to students at his school. He buys **2000 ladoos** for the event. However, not all students attend the celebration.

If xxx students attend the event, Chef gives **one ladoo to each attending student**.

Help Chef determine **how many ladoos are left undistributed**.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecountRemainingLadooscountRemainingLadooscountRemainingLadoos – This function calculates the number of ladoos left after distributing one ladoo to each attending student.

Parameters
xxx : The number of students who attended the event.
Return Value
Returns a number representing the number of ladoos left after distribution.
Constraints
1≤x<20001 \leq x < 20001≤x<2000

Input Format

A single integer xxx denoting the number of students who attended the event.


Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer — the number of ladoos remaining with Chef after distribution.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer x (1 ≤ x < 2000) – the number of students who attended the event.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of ladoos remaining after giving one ladoo to each attending student.

solution.approach: Chef always has 2000 ladoos. For each test case we read the number of attending students x and compute the remaining ladoos as 2000 - x. This is a direct O(1) arithmetic operation per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time subtraction for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used regardless of T.

code.Python3:
import sys

data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
idx = 1
for _ in range(T):
    x = int(data[idx])
    idx += 1
    out.append(str(2000 - x))
print('\n'.join(out))
